raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"Section advances an explicit contribution claim ('no prior work has used RL directly to optimize calibration in the LLM setting') and a gap statement relative to supervised approaches; verifying both are accurately and defensibly scoped is a central review task.","R1.2":"The section constructs a causal chain (Brier reward → lower ECE → cross-domain transfer) and an ablation argument (Brier vs. log-loss); internal logical consistency of these arguments—including whether the cross-domain result actually supports the transfer claim—requires scrutiny.","R1.3":"Numerous specific quantitative claims are load-bearing: ECE 0.18→0.06 (TriviaQA), 0.21→0.09 (MMLU), accuracy within 0.4% of SFT baseline, ECE below 0.10 on all OOD splits, 1.7% accuracy drop under log-loss. If any of these are unsupported by referenced experiments or tables, the section's credibility collapses.","R1.4":"The novelty claim is a strong universal ('to our knowledge, no prior work'); verifying its accuracy against adjacent literature—calibration-shaped rewards, RLHF with uncertainty objectives, calibration of generative models—is a core review responsibility.","R1.7":"Five citation clusters anchor the background and contrast claims (Christiano 2017, Ouyang 2022, Kadavath 2022, Tian 2023, Lin 2022); confirming that years, authors, and attributed findings are accurate and that key competing work is not omitted is standard review scope."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review prioritize technical and scientific correctness—verifying quantitative claims, novelty scope, and logical reasoning—or editorial quality—writing clarity, sentence-level polish, and narrative flow?"}

rationale.R1.1: Section advances an explicit contribution claim ('no prior work has used RL directly to optimize calibration in the LLM setting') and a gap statement relative to supervised approaches; verifying both are accurately and defensibly scoped is a central review task.
rationale.R1.2: The section constructs a causal chain (Brier reward → lower ECE → cross-domain transfer) and an ablation argument (Brier vs. log-loss); internal logical consistency of these arguments—including whether the cross-domain result actually supports the transfer claim—requires scrutiny.
rationale.R1.3: Numerous specific quantitative claims are load-bearing: ECE 0.18→0.06 (TriviaQA), 0.21→0.09 (MMLU), accuracy within 0.4% of SFT baseline, ECE below 0.10 on all OOD splits, 1.7% accuracy drop under log-loss. If any of these are unsupported by referenced experiments or tables, the section's credibility collapses.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty claim is a strong universal ('to our knowledge, no prior work'); verifying its accuracy against adjacent literature—calibration-shaped rewards, RLHF with uncertainty objectives, calibration of generative models—is a core review responsibility.
rationale.R1.7: Five citation clusters anchor the background and contrast claims (Christiano 2017, Ouyang 2022, Kadavath 2022, Tian 2023, Lin 2022); confirming that years, authors, and attributed findings are accurate and that key competing work is not omitted is standard review scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize technical and scientific correctness—verifying quantitative claims, novelty scope, and logical reasoning—or editorial quality—writing clarity, sentence-level polish, and narrative flow?
